zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen於1197年2月28日出生在德國貝也纽伦堡時，她的父親Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König18歲，母親Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin25歲。她於1219年11月30日與Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她於1204年住在可特格希 貝也雷札特可萊斯。她去世於1235年11月5日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安薩莫拉托羅享年38歲，埋葬在西班牙安達盧希亞澤維利亞澤維利亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen于1197年2月28日出生在德国貝也纽伦堡时，她的父亲Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König18岁，母亲Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin25岁。她于1219年11月30日与Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她于1204年住在可特格希 貝也雷札特可萊斯。她去世于1235年11月5日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安薩莫拉托羅享年38岁，埋葬在西班牙安達盧希亞澤維利亞澤維利亞。

nameMeanings.name: House
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name for someone employed ‘at the house’, from Middle English hous(e) (Old English hūs), probably a religious house such as a convent. In the Middle Ages the majority of the population lived in cottages or huts rather than houses.
English: variant of   Howes  .

English: perhaps a topographic name from an unrecorded Middle English huse, a southwest dialect form of Old English hyse ‘place overgrown with (water) plants’, a derivative of Old English hos ‘(water) plant’. In modern English, the name might have been absorbed into Huss or Hose .

variants.name: Huizenga
meanings: West Frisian and Dutch (mainly Groningen): habitational name from Huizinge in Groningen, a placename possibly composed with Middle Dutch huus, huse, Dutch huis ‘house’ (see House ).

variants.name: Houze
meanings: Altered form of   House   4, a surname of German origin (see   Haus  ).
French (Houzé): metonymic occupational name for a maker or seller of boots and shoes or a nickname for someone noted for his footwear, from Old French ho(u)se ‘boot, shoe’.

variants.name: Howes
meanings: English:

pronounced to rhyme with hose, a variant of Howe 1 with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. Hose (Leicestershire), recorded as Howes in 1086, is named with the plural of Old English hōh ‘hill spur’.
pronounced to rhyme with cows, a variant of Howe 2 (see Hugh ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Howse
meanings: English (Oxfordshire):
variant of   House  .
variants of   Howes  .

variants.name: Houseman
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English hous ‘house’ + man ‘man’, denoting someone employed at ‘the house’, probably a religious house or convent. Compare House .
English: altered form of Oulsnam (itself a variant of Wolstenholme ), with metathesis of final -nam to -man and development of inorganic initial H-.
Americanized form of German   Hausmann   or Dutch   Huisman  .

variants.name: Huse
meanings: North German: from Middle Low German hūs ‘house’ (see House 1), which may also refer to an important building such as the town hall, a castle, or a manor house, so that the name would indicate dwelling in close proximity to these, or perhaps it is an epithet for a servant who worked at a great house.
English (Berkshire): variant of   Hose   and possibly a shortened form of   Hussey  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen was born on 28 February 1197, in Nuremberg, Bavaria, Germany, her father, Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König, was 18 and her mother, Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin, was 25. She married Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León on 30 November 1219, in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. She lived in Rezat District, Bavaria in 1204. She died on 5 November 1235, in Toro, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spain, at the age of 38, and was buried in Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalusia, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen est née le 28 février 1197, à Nürnberg, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León le 30 novembre 1219, à Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Elle a vécu à Rezatkreis, Königreich Bayern en 1204. Elle est décédée le 5 novembre 1235, à Toro, Zamora, Castilla y León, Espagne, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterrée à Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalucía, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen am 28. Februar 1197 in Nürnberg, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König 18 und ihre Mutter Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin 25. Sie hat am 30. November 1219 in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1204 hat sie in Rezatkreis, Königreich Bayern gelebt. Sie starb am 5. November 1235 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Toro, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spanien und wurde in Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalucía, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen è nata il il 28 febbraio 1197 nel Nürnberg, Bayern, Germania, sua padre, Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König, aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León il 30 novembre 1219, nel Burgos, Castilla y León, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 3 figlie. Vissuto a Rezatkreis, Königreich Bayern nel 1204. Morì il 5 novembre 1235, nel Toro, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spagna, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolta a Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalucía, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufenが1197年2月28日にドイツバイエルンンuルンバーグで生まれた時，父Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher Königは18歳，母Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferköniginは25歳であった。1219年11月30日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンブルゴスでFernando III Rey de Castilla y de Leónと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1204年にはクoニグレイチ バイエルンレザトクレイスに住んでいた。彼女は1235年11月5日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンザモーアトロで38歳で死去し，スペインアンダルクiアセヴィラセヴィラにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen(이)가 1197년 2월 28일 독일 베이른 뉘른베르크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1219년 11월 30일 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 버고스에서 Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1204년도에 ko니그레이치 베이른 레자트크레이스에서 살았다. 그녀는 1235년 11월 5일 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 자모라 토로에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 앤다루ci아 세비라 세비라에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen nasceu em 28 de fevereiro de 1197, em Nürnberg, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León em 30 de novembro de 1219, em Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela viveu em Rezatkreis, Königreich Bayern em 1204. Ela faleceu em 5 de novembro de 1235, em Toro, Zamora, Castilla y León, Espanha, com 38 anos, e foi sepultada em Sevilla, Sevilla, Andaluzia, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen родилась 28 февраля 1197 г. в местности Нюрнберг, беиирн, Германия, её отцу по имени Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León 30 ноября 1219 г. в местности бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она жила в местности резаткрис, кoниграич беиирн в 1204 году. Она умерла 5 ноября 1235 г. в местности торо, замора, кастилла и леoн, Испания в возрасте 38 лет и была похоронена в местности севилла, севилла, андалукiа, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elisabeth of Hohenstaufen House of Hohenstaufen nació el 28 de febrero de 1197, en Nürnberg, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Philipp von Schwaben römisch-deutscher König, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Irene von Byzanz deutsche Stauferkönigin, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León el 30 de noviembre de 1219, en Burgos, Castilla y León, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Rezatkreis, Königreich Bayern en 1204. Murió el 5 de noviembre de 1235, en Toro, Zamora, Castilla y León, España, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultada en Sevilla, Sevilla, Andalucía, España.
